title: The Bob Next Door

jokes.text: Mmm. (drools) at mention of cooking books and fudging numbers
jokes.setup: Mayor discusses financial crisis using accounting metaphors
jokes.punchline: Homer literally salivates at food-related words
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer character beat - his food obsession overrides serious context. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Good morning, children. Today, we are instituting shortened school hours. How shortened? Take 'em home, Otto.
jokes.setup: Budget cuts lead to shortened school hours
jokes.punchline: School day is so short it ends immediately
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from minor budget cuts to complete absurdity. Clean structure and timing.

jokes.text: I don't know, Chief. We can't afford a radar gun, so we're just using a thermos on top of a hose handle.
jokes.setup: Police need to check speeding in budget crisis
jokes.punchline: Makeshift radar gun is obviously fake household items
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific visual absurdity. The thermos/hose handle combo is perfectly ridiculous yet believable for Springfield.

jokes.text: Well, it's better than my pistol. Hey, hey, uh, that Ralphie's getting to be a pretty fine artist. I drew it.
jokes.setup: Chief compliments Ralph's artwork on the fake radar gun
jokes.punchline: Lou reveals he drew it, not Ralph
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Wiggum's cluelessness and Lou's understated competence.

jokes.text: What if someone moves in with two Barts? Or four teenage Barts?
jokes.setup: Marge worries about new neighbors
jokes.punchline: Escalating nightmare scenario of multiple Barts
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation that plays on Marge's anxiety and acknowledges Bart as a handful.

jokes.text: Eh, at least they're learning their fractions.
jokes.setup: Teen Barts are doing something destructive with engines
jokes.punchline: Homer finds educational silver lining
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - missing the point completely while trying to be optimistic.

jokes.text: What kind of big, fat moron would fall for... (sniffs) Cookies! So delicious. Must buy house.
jokes.setup: Homer mocks people who fall for cookie smell house-selling trick
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls for it himself
jokes.explanation: Textbook Homer moment - his hypocrisy and lack of self-awareness perfectly executed with immediate payoff.

jokes.text: Homer, no! Marge, get loan preapproved. Offer over asking. Waive inspections. Two-day escrow! Initial the radon disclosure. And done.
jokes.setup: Homer is under cookie spell wanting to buy house
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire real estate jargon showing complete commitment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's impulsiveness with authentic real estate terminology.

jokes.text: But my loan has already been sold in pieces to banks, hedge funds and municipalities across the globe.
jokes.setup: Homer loses house to another buyer
jokes.punchline: His loan is already part of complex financial instruments
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of 2008 financial crisis and predatory lending practices.

jokes.text: Death to Homer! At least we'll always have Beowulf. That's not us.
jokes.setup: Someone threatens Homer over the loan situation
jokes.punchline: Homer confuses death threats with classic literature
jokes.explanation: Random but funny - Homer's cultural confusion adds layer to the chaos.

jokes.text: A credenza? Credenza?! Lowboy? Sideboard? Portmanteau?
jokes.setup: Homer sees fancy furniture being moved in
jokes.punchline: Increasingly pretentious furniture names trigger him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's class insecurity through furniture terminology.

jokes.text: Mom, it's so superficial to judge our new neighbor based on his furniture. (gasps) He drives a hybrid!
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizes superficial judgment
jokes.punchline: Immediately judges based on different superficial thing
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing even Lisa has her biases, just different ones.

jokes.text: And where there's exotic fish, there's got to be a... Water feature. Dames. (groans)
jokes.setup: Marge and Lisa get excited about fancy yard features
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive 'Dames' reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer eye-roll moment at female enthusiasm.

jokes.text: No doubt you read about me in your nuisance neighbor disclosure.
jokes.setup: Bart introducing himself to new neighbor
jokes.punchline: He's so notorious there'd be legal disclosure requirements
jokes.explanation: Clever joke that builds on real estate disclosure laws while establishing Bart's reputation

jokes.text: Well, I'm gonna go introduce myself Bart Simpson-style. (hawks and spits)
jokes.setup: Bart announces his introduction method
jokes.punchline: Spitting as signature move
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Bart defining his own brand of rudeness.

jokes.text: Hello, Bart! Hello, Bart. Hello, BART. (screams) Sideshow Bob!
jokes.setup: Repeated hellos with increasing emphasis
jokes.punchline: Recognition and terror
jokes.explanation: Great reveal moment with perfect escalation and character dynamics. Classic Sideshow Bob entrance.

jokes.text: Side what whom?
jokes.setup: Bart accuses him of being Sideshow Bob
jokes.punchline: Fake confusion with awkward grammar
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that sells the disguise attempt.

jokes.text: A lot of people sound like Sideshow Bob. Like Frasier on Cheers. Or Frasier on Frasier. Or Lieutenant Commander Tom Dodge in Down Periscope.
jokes.setup: Homer explains why voice similarity doesn't matter
jokes.punchline: Listing Kelsey Grammer roles including obscure movie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke acknowledging the voice actor while staying in character. Down Periscope reference is perfectly obscure.

jokes.text: Ice cream?! Oh, nothing is ever boobs or ice cream.
jokes.setup: Homer reacts excitedly to ice cream truck sound
jokes.punchline: Reveals his perpetual disappointment pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Homer's optimism and disappointment cycle. The pairing of 'boobs or ice cream' is quintessentially Homer.

jokes.text: Sorry, gentlemen. I'm not a fan of that particular style of music. My radio dial never strays from 89.4 'The Spur.'
jokes.setup: Gilbert and Sullivan test to expose Bob
jokes.punchline: Claims to only like country music
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bob's discipline in maintaining his disguise, fighting his cultured nature.

jokes.text: Calm, Comfortable, a 'Cuddly Puppies' calendar. Aw, little guy can't get out of the pumpkin.
jokes.setup: Bart searches for evidence Bob is evil
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by cute puppy calendar
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat showing even focused Bart gets distracted by cute things.

jokes.text: Who needs a knife this big? It's probably a deboner. (chuckles) Boner.
jokes.setup: Bart finds suspicious large knife
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent explanation leads to juvenile humor
jokes.explanation: Standard juvenile wordplay - functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Pretty lady in a strapless dress coming through! Oh, yeah! Come here, woman! If you were my wife, I never would've killed you!
jokes.setup: Prisoners catcalling visiting Marge
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about not murdering her
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works by subverting expected catcalls with murder reference.

jokes.text: Thank you. You're very sweet.
jokes.setup: Prisoner says he wouldn't have killed her
jokes.punchline: Marge politely thanks him for not planning murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - her politeness in completely inappropriate situations is beautifully executed here.

jokes.text: Nothing is holding up the dress! Only her melons!
jokes.setup: Prisoners continue crude commentary
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about support and anatomy
jokes.explanation: Fairly crude and obvious double entendre without much cleverness.

jokes.text: Guys, guys, I'm married. You are? So sorry.
jokes.setup: Marge tries to shut down catcalls by mentioning marriage
jokes.punchline: Prisoners sincerely apologize as if that changes everything
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectations - the prisoners showing unexpected respectfulness toward marriage.

jokes.text: Hope I didn't interrupt your petite dejeuner.
jokes.setup: Bob greeting Marge in the morning
jokes.punchline: Uses pretentious French for 'breakfast', revealing his true cultured nature
jokes.explanation: Good character slip showing Bob's pretensions breaking through his disguise

jokes.text: They're playing Spokane. The Cascades? They totally stole the Federal Cup from us last year.
jokes.setup: Bob mentions baseball game to lure Bart
jokes.punchline: Fake sports rivalry with made-up teams and trophy
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about fictional sports teams and manufactured rivalry

jokes.text: Marge, the bathroom scale is lying again.
jokes.setup: Homer weighing himself
jokes.punchline: Blames scale instead of his weight
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer denial joke - functional character moment.

jokes.text: (high-pitched voice): I'm not Sideshow Bob. I'm the real Walt Warren. But that's impossible. If you're the real Walt Warren, who's taking our son to the baseball game right now?
jokes.setup: Family discovers real Walt tied up
jokes.punchline: Realization that fake Walt has Bart
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation moment with proper setup and payoff structure.

jokes.text: It's like you're a Chinese empress.
jokes.setup: Bob shows his small feet from face transplant
jokes.punchline: Comparison to foot binding practice
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference that explains the visual absurdity of Bob's small feet.

jokes.text: Say, Bob, why do you keep measuring my face? Just passing the time, bunky. Well, I guess it beats what the last guy did.
jokes.setup: Walt notices Bob's suspicious behavior
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of even worse previous cellmate
jokes.explanation: Dark prison humor that sets up the face transplant while hinting at worse possibilities.

jokes.text: Five-second rule.
jokes.setup: Bob drops Walt's severed face
jokes.punchline: Applies food rule to human face
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor applying mundane rule to horrific situation. Perfect economy and originality.

jokes.text: Luckily, as Krusty's sidekick, I'd been hit with so many pies that my face had lost all sensation. Or so I thought. (screams in pain)
jokes.setup: Bob explains why face removal would be easy
jokes.punchline: Reality of pain contradicts his theory
jokes.explanation: Excellent integration of character history with current plot, plus good misdirection and payoff.

jokes.text: $143,000. Oh! It was only $27,000 when you came in, but we invested it well. Would you like a commission? Not allowed.
jokes.setup: Bob gets Walt's belongings back
jokes.punchline: Prison somehow made great investment returns
jokes.explanation: Absurd premise that prison system is somehow great at investing, with bureaucratic punchline.

jokes.text: Selling the house will be murder. You could just rent it till the market recovers... which'll be never.
jokes.setup: Bob plans to kill Bart then sell house
jokes.punchline: Casual real estate advice mixed with economic pessimism
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'murder' meaning both killing and difficult task, plus topical housing market reference.

jokes.text: I tried to warn you by writing 'Bart Simpson will die' on the walls, but you ignored me. You have to admit, 'Bart Simpson Will Die' was kind of open to interpretation.
jokes.setup: Walt explains his failed warning attempt
jokes.punchline: Bart points out the warning wasn't exactly subtle
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about how obvious death threats might be missed as vague warnings.

jokes.text: My face. My beautiful, borrowed face.
jokes.setup: Bob's face starts falling off
jokes.punchline: Vain concern for stolen face's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bob's vanity even about his stolen face.

jokes.text: I don't care if you don't have a face. I just need something warm to cuddle at night!
jokes.setup: Bob's face falls off in front of waitress
jokes.punchline: She's desperate enough not to care about no face
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about loneliness being so extreme that facial features become optional.

jokes.text: Walt, it's a nice face, but I don't think it would drive a professional waitress to lie.
jokes.setup: Walt thinks waitress lied because she loves his face
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutal assessment of face's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - tactlessly honest while trying to be helpful.

jokes.text: All the good men are either gay or have no face.
jokes.setup: Joyce lamenting her dating options
jokes.punchline: Includes 'no face' as common dating dealbreaker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of common dating complaint by adding completely absurd 'no face' category.

jokes.text: I'll probably kill myself pretty soon with all the stupid things I do. Oh, no! I swallowed my tongue! (gagging)
jokes.setup: Bart argues Bob doesn't need to kill him
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates by choking on tongue
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - Bart immediately proves his point about being accident-prone.

jokes.text: But their sum total is the greatest murder since Snape killed Dumbledore. Oh, I haven't gotten to that part yet. It's a four-year-old book. I'm a slow reader.
jokes.setup: Bob spoils Harry Potter while explaining his plan
jokes.punchline: Bart's embarrassed admission about reading speed
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about spoilers plus character moment showing Bart's academic struggles.

jokes.text: Sí. Uh, yes, I'd like... um... How do you say taco in Mexican? Taco. Yes, that's exactly the word I want to translate. Taco.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to order food in Mexico
jokes.punchline: Doesn't realize taco is the same word in both languages
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer cultural ignorance moment - functional character comedy.

jokes.text: Hold it right there, Switches of Eastface. Switches of Eastface? I don't want to censor myself. That's how creativity dies.
jokes.setup: Wiggum tries to say 'son of a bitch'
jokes.punchline: Bob's pretentious defense of free speech
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Bob's intellectual pretensions even during arrest, plus Wiggum's creative cursing.

jokes.text: You see, Bob, Bart was never convinced you were Walt. So before he left for the game, he called us and warned us to track you. Which we were able to do through the dashboard of you car, which, like all hybrids, is secretly monitored by the government.
jokes.setup: Wiggum explains how they found Bob
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of government surveillance of eco-friendly cars
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid conspiracy humor that ties back to earlier hybrid joke.

jokes.text: We're takin' you in. But first, we're gonna make a stop at the shore for some Skee-Ball and funnel cakes.
jokes.setup: Multi-jurisdictional arrest at Five Corners
jokes.punchline: New Jersey cops prioritize boardwalk fun over police work
jokes.explanation: Good regional stereotype humor about New Jersey shore culture

jokes.text: Well, not everything. Apparently someone switched your face with a butt.
jokes.setup: Marge says she's glad everything's okay
jokes.punchline: Bart points out Walt still has Bob's unattractive face
jokes.explanation: Good juvenile insult that acknowledges the visual absurdity of the face swap.

jokes.text: Howdilly-dowdilly, next-door-oonies. Howdy, Cousin Ted. Howdy do to you, Cousin Ned. How are Roddy and Toddy? Flantastic times two. How are Connie and Bonnie? Dandy as candy, like every good Flandy.
jokes.setup: New neighbors move in next door
jokes.punchline: Entire extended Flanders family with rhyming speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Flanders' annoying verbal tics multiplied across entire family.

tmdb_overview: Bart becomes convinced that his new neighbor, Walt, is his archenemy, Sideshow Bob, disguised and back for revenge. But when Marge tries to convince Bart otherwise by taking him to visit the state penitentiary, a disturbing truth is revealed.
